23 de Junio · Day's Intake

Today's Findings

Ten pieces logged in a single sitting — seven new plates for Costa Rica Histórica, three new entries for El Oasis. Laid out here as found, before filing.

7Postcard Catalogues
3El Oasis Entries
10Total Pieces
I.

Costa Rica Histórica

Postcards & Ephemera Archive

Seven self-contained catalogues from the encyclopedia-in-progress — bridges, river crossings, steamship advertising, grinding stones, devil masks, and a pilgrimage in two postcards.

1 / 7

Puente sobre el Virilla

The Virilla Bridge, in Two Hands

Two early postcard views of the same iron railway crossing on the Pacific line — one a plain commercial photograph, the other a manuscript-inscribed engineer's memento.

2 PlatesFerrocarril al Pacífico
2 / 7

Crossings

Three Fords, Three Eras

A barefoot ford on the Atlantic slope, an oxcart team in a Pacific-side current, and a motorcar nosing through a Guanacaste streambed — the old negotiation between traveler and river.

3 PlatesTransport Series
3 / 7

El Reventazón y la Línea a Limón

The Reventazón Gorge & the San José–Limón Line

Postcard views of the Reventazón gorge and the rail corridor it threads — the Atlantic-slope counterpart to the Pacific railway, c. early 20th century.

Multi-PlateRail Corridor Series
4 / 7

The Great White Fleet

La Gran Flota Blanca

Postcards, advertisements, and ephemera documenting the United Fruit Company's steamship line — the vessel that carried bananas north, and tourists (and an idea of the tropics) south.

Multi-PlateMaritime & Commerce Series
5 / 7

El Metate y la Mano

Pacific Carved Form, Atlantic River Stone

A footed, carved tripod metate from the Pacific Chorotega-adjacent tradition set beside unworked river boulders from the Talamanca Atlantic slope — same tool, two cultural spheres.

2 PlatesMaterial Culture Series
6 / 7

Máscaras del Diablito

Boruca / Brunca — Térraba-Boruca, Puntarenas

A 500-year-old act of defiance, carved in balsa — and the twenty-five years in which it became Costa Rica's most recognizable souvenir.

2 PlatesIndigenous Material Culture
7 / 7

La Negrita

Nuestra Señora de los Ángeles — Santuario y Romería

A small stone figure found on a riverside rock in 1635 became, within two centuries, the patroness of a nation — traced here through two early postcards of the Cartago basílica and procession.

StandalonePilgrimage & Devotion Series
II.

El Oasis Property

Garden, Grounds & Community

Three entries for the El Oasis collections — a natural history plate, an ornamental features catalogue, and the tsunami signage record.

1 / 3

Rasgos Ornamentales

Ornamental & Decorative Features

A catalogue of the carved spheres, garden statuary, and decorative stonework scattered through El Oasis's grounds — the property's smaller, easily-missed details.

Garden & GroundsEl Oasis Collections
2 / 3

Señalización de Tsunami

Official Evacuation & Warning Signage

Official SINAMOT/CNE evacuation signage installed along the Playa Hermosa coastline, documented alongside one unaffiliated directional sign for contrast.

Community RecordEl Oasis Collections
3 / 3
Plate image not yet supplied
(brahman_cow_plate.jpg)

Brahman Cow & Calves

Other Life — Natural History Collections

A mature Brahman cow and two calves at a roadside fence line, Playa Hermosa — the breed's working papers traced from India, through Brazil, to a Texas registry, to this fence.

Other LifeImage pending — brahman_cow_plate.jpg not yet supplied
